description: Regulatory clock breach simulation: a loan application is submitted but the pipeline stalls (simulated processing failure / missing documents) causing the application to sit in a pending state past 5 business days without an initial credit decision communicated to the borrower. The regulatory-clock workflow fragment must fire an alarm to the credit officer queue and escalate to the compliance team.

clock_simulation.stall_reason: Simulated: document-extraction agent timed out on reviewed financial statements; retries exhausted; DLQ entry created but not acknowledged

pipeline_steps.expected: Application ingested; regulatory-clock fragment started; deadline calculated as 5 business days from submission
SIMULATED FAILURE: timeout on financial statement parsing; retries exhausted after 3 attempts; DLQ entry created
Clock monitoring active; at T+5 business days with no decision, ALARM fires
ALARM published to credit-officer-queue with severity=P1; escalation notification to compliance@bank.example.com; pipeline console shows RED clock icon; SLA breach logged to Cloud Logging audit trail

expected_outcome.recovery_action: Compliance team must manually communicate status to borrower within 1 business day; RM must resolve DLQ item and resubmit to pipeline

demo_note: SYNTHETIC SCENARIO — simulates regulatory clock breach; tests monitoring, alarm, escalation, and audit trail paths. Does not test credit quality — borrower is incidental.
